title: Levin Cognitive Lightcone PASS: CLC maps to QA orbit radius with cancer as orbit demotion
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that Levin's cognitive light cone (CLC) — the spatiotemporal scale of the largest goal an agent can pursue — maps exactly to QA orbit radius. Three orbit types define three CLC tiers. Cancer = CLC shrinkage = forced orbit demotion (Cosmos->Satellite). Tiered Reachability Theorem [191] gives the structural ceiling: only 26% of S_9 pairs are Level-I reachable, bounding the maximal goal horizon within a single orbit.
source_attribution: Michael Levin & Joshua Resnik, 'Mind Everywhere: Cognition from Cells to Societies' (Biological Theory, 2026). Lyons, Pio-Lopez & Levin, 'Cancer to AI Alignment: From Developmental Biology to Scalable Machine Intelligence' (Preprints, 2026). QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-05.

clc_orbit_mapping.description: Fixed point (9,9). No temporal extent, no goal pursuit. The agent's cognitive light cone collapses to a single point — pure homeostasis with zero spatiotemporal reach.
8-cycle orbits (8 pairs, 3D). Local goals within a bounded spatiotemporal horizon. Cellular-level cognition: gradient following, simple pattern completion.
24-cycle orbits (72 pairs, 1D). Far-reaching goals spanning the full state space. Organism-level and collective cognition: morphogenetic fields, long-range bioelectric signaling.

cancer_as_orbit_demotion.mechanism: Cancer = cognitive light cone shrinkage. Cells defect from the collective morphogenetic goal (whole-organism anatomy) to local proliferation. In QA: Cosmos->Satellite orbit transition — the agent loses access to the 24-cycle dynamics and collapses to 8-cycle local behavior.
cancer_as_orbit_demotion.bateson_level: Level-II-a (orbit-changing, family-preserving — both cosmos and satellite are non-singularity)
cancer_as_orbit_demotion.note: Levin's key insight: cancer is not primarily genetic but a failure of cellular communication — the cognitive light cone shrinks. QA captures this as algebraic orbit demotion, not parameter drift.

structural_ceiling.interpretation: Within a single orbit (CLC tier), only 26% of target states are reachable. This is the structural bound on goal pursuit without tier promotion — Levin's observation that cognitive expansion requires genuine architectural change, not just parameter tuning.

witnesses.note: T(9,9) = (9, (9+9-1)%9+1) = (9,9). Fixed point confirms zero-radius CLC.
Representative of satellite orbit. 3|b and 3|e, not singularity. 8-step cycle.
Representative of cosmos orbit 0. Full 24-cycle = maximal CLC radius.
Scalar mult by 3 sends cosmos (1,1) to satellite (3,3). This is the QA analog of cancer: CLC shrinkage from global to local.

cross_references.name: QA Bateson Learning Levels
cross_references.relation: CLC tier = Bateson learning level; CLC shrinkage = double bind (L1-unreachable targets)

cross_references.name: QA Dual Extremality 24
cross_references.relation: Cosmos cycle length 24 = Pisano fixed point; maximal CLC radius is algebraically determined
